title: Standardized World Income Inequality Database (SWIID) — Version 9.92
units: Gini index (0-100 scale), disposable and market income, with standard errors
notes: SWIID provides multiply-imputed Gini estimates standardized for comparability across ~196 countries, using a Bayesian latent variable model that draws on hundreds of underlying source series (LIS, Eurostat, national statistical offices, etc.) per country. Downloaded via the Harvard Dataverse API (dataset doi:10.7910/DVN/LM4OWF, 'The Standardized World Income Inequality Database, Versions 8-9' — the Dataverse landing page title is stale but the dataset itself is kept current; latest file swiid9_92.zip was released 2026-04-07, matching the version cited on fsolt.org as of retrieval). The Dataverse landing page itself (dataset.xhtml) returned an empty HTTP 202 body when fetched directly with curl (likely a JS/anti-bot challenge, similar to the Maddison Project block noted elsewhere in this repo), but the underlying Dataverse REST API (/api/datasets/:persistentId/) was NOT blocked and returned full JSON with a direct file-download link, so no Wayback Machine fallback was needed. Archive contains: swiid9_92.dta (Stata, full multiply-imputed data, 100 imputations x observations), swiid9_92.rda (R), swiid9_92_summary.csv (point estimates + SEs, 1 row per country-year — this is the practically useful CSV), plus PDF codebooks for Stata and R usage. Iran's gini_disp (disposable-income Gini) series runs continuously 1969-2023, e.g. 43.9 in 1969 declining to 35.4 in 2023 (gini_mkt/market-income Gini also provided, consistently ~2.8-3 points higher, reflecting redistribution). Useful for cross-checking WID.world (World Inequality Database) top-income-share figures for Iran, which measure a related but distinct inequality concept.
